100→     * Status logic:
   101→     * - OK: Age <= threshold
   102→     * - EXCEPTED: Age > threshold BUT has active exception
   103→     * - OVERDUE: Age > threshold AND no active exception
   104→     *
   105→     * Feature 021: Overdue status calculation
   106→     *
   107→     * PERFORMANCE: When calling this method in a loop, pass a pre-loaded list of
   108→     * active exceptions to avoid N+1 queries. Use getActiveExceptions() to get
   109→     * the cached list.
   110→     *
   111→     * @param vulnerability The vulnerability to check
   112→     * @param asset The asset the vulnerability is on
   113→     * @param activeExceptions Optional pre-loaded list of active exceptions (avoids N+1)
   114→     * @return OverdueInfo with complete overdue status information
   115→     */
   116→    fun calculateOverdueStatus(
   117→        vulnerability: Vulnerability,
   118→        asset: Asset,
   119→        activeExceptions: List<VulnerabilityException>? = null
   120→    ): OverdueInfo {
   121→        // Get configured threshold
   122→        val reminderOneDays = vulnerabilityConfigService.getReminderOneDays()
   123→
   124→        // Calculate age in days from scan timestamp to now
   125→        val ageInDays = ChronoUnit.DAYS.between(vulnerability.scanTimestamp, LocalDateTime.now()).toInt()
   126→
   127→        // Use pre-loaded exceptions or fetch from cache
   128→        val exceptions = activeExceptions ?: getActiveExceptions()
   129→
   130→        val matchingException = exceptions.firstOrNull { exception ->
   131→            exception.matches(vulnerability, asset)
   132→        }
   133→
   134→        // Determine overdue status
   135→        val isOverdue = ageInDays > reminderOneDays
   136→        val status = when {
   137→            matchingException != null -> OverdueStatus.EXCEPTED  // Has exception, regardless of age
   138→            isOverdue -> OverdueStatus.OVERDUE                    // Overdue and no exception
   139→            else -> OverdueStatus.OK                               // Within threshold
   140→        }
   141→
   142→        // Calculate days overdue (only if actually overdue after considering exceptions)
   143→        val daysOverdue = if (status == OverdueStatus.OVERDUE) {
   144→            ageInDays - reminderOneDays
   145→        } else {
   146→            null
   147→        }
   148→
   149→        log.trace("Vulnerability {} on asset {}: age={}d, threshold={}d, status={}, exception={}",
   150→            vulnerability.id, asset.id, ageInDays, reminderOneDays, status, matchingException?.id)
   151→
   152→        return OverdueInfo(
   153→            status = status,
   154→            ageInDays = ageInDays,
   155→            daysOverdue = daysOverdue,
   156→            exceptionId = matchingException?.id,
   157→            exceptionReason = matchingException?.reason,
   158→            exceptionEndDate = matchingException?.expirationDate
   159→        )
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    /**
   163→     * Get current vulnerabilities (latest scan per asset) with exception status
   164→     *
   165→     * @param severity Optional CVSS severity filter (e.g., "Critical", "High")
   166→     * @param system Optional system/asset name filter
   167→     * @param exceptionStatus Optional filter: "excepted", "not_excepted", "overdue", "ok", or null (all)
   168→     * @param product Optional product/version filter (substring match)
   169→     * @param adDomain Optional AD domain filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
   170→     * @param page Page number (0-indexed)
   171→     * @param size Page size (default 50)
   172→     * @return Paginated list of vulnerabilities with exception information
   173→     */
   174→    fun getCurrentVulnerabilities(
   175→        severity: String? = null,
   176→        system: String? = null,
   177→        exceptionStatus: String? = null,
   178→        product: String? = null,
   179→        adDomain: String? = null,
   180→        page: Int = 0,
   181→        size: Int = 50
   182→    ): Pair<List<VulnerabilityWithExceptionDto>, Long> {
   183→        log.debug("Getting current vulnerabilities - severity: {}, system: {}, exceptionStatus: {}, product: {}, adDomain: {}, page: {}, size: {}",
   184→            severity, system, exceptionStatus, product, adDomain, page, size)
   185→
   186→        // OPTIMIZATION: Use database query instead of loading all vulnerabilities into memory
   187→        // This is significantly faster for large datasets (100K+ vulnerabilities)
   188→        val pageable = io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable.from(page, size)
   189→
   190→        // Use optimized query that filters at database level
   191→        val currentVulnsPage = vulnerabilityRepository.findLatestVulnerabilitiesPerAssetWithFilters(
   192→            severity = severity?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   193→            assetName = system?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   194→            productFilter = product?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   195→            domainFilter = adDomain?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   196→            cloudAccountIdFilter = null, // Not supported in this legacy function
   197→            pageable = io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable.UNPAGED // Get all for exception status filtering
   198→        )
   199→
   200→        val currentVulns = currentVulnsPage.content
   201→        log.debug("Found {} current vulnerabilities from database", currentVulns.size)
   202→
   203→        // PERFORMANCE: Get active exceptions ONCE (cached for 5 minutes)
   204→        // This replaces the inefficient findAll().filter{} pattern
   205→        val activeExceptions = getActiveExceptions()
   206→
   207→        log.debug("Found {} active exceptions (from cache)", activeExceptions.size)
   208→
   209→        // Map to DTOs with exception information and overdue status
   210→        val vulnsWithException = currentVulns.map { vuln ->
   211→            // Calculate overdue status - pass pre-loaded exceptions to avoid N+1 queries
   212→            val overdueInfo = calculateOverdueStatus(vuln, vuln.asset, activeExceptions)
   213→
   214→            // Check if any active exception matches this vulnerability
   215→            val matchingException = activeExceptions.firstOrNull { exception ->
   216→                exception.matches(vuln, vuln.asset)
   217→            }
   218→
   219→            val hasException = matchingException != null
   220→            val exceptionReason = matchingException?.reason
   221→
   222→            VulnerabilityWithExceptionDto(
   223→                id = vuln.id,
   224→                asset = vuln.asset,  // Include full asset entity
   225→                assetId = vuln.asset.id!!,
   226→                assetName = vuln.asset.name,
   227→                assetIp = vuln.asset.ip,
   228→                vulnerabilityId = vuln.vulnerabilityId,
   229→                cvssSeverity = vuln.cvssSeverity,
   230→                vulnerableProductVersions = vuln.vulnerableProductVersions,
   231→                daysOpen = vuln.daysOpen,
   232→                scanTimestamp = vuln.scanTimestamp,
   233→                hasException = hasException,
   234→                exceptionReason = exceptionReason,
   235→                // Feature 021: Add overdue status fields
   236→                ageInDays = overdueInfo.ageInDays,
   237→                overdueStatus = overdueInfo.status,
   238→                daysOverdue = overdueInfo.daysOverdue,
   239→                exceptionId = overdueInfo.exceptionId,
   240→                exceptionEndDate = overdueInfo.exceptionEndDate
   241→            )
   242→        }
   243→
   244→        // Apply exception status filter (support both hyphen and underscore formats)
   245→        // Feature 021: Enhanced to support overdue status filtering
   246→        val normalizedExceptionStatus = exceptionStatus?.lowercase()?.replace("-", "_")
   247→        val result = when (normalizedExceptionStatus) {
   248→            "excepted" -> vulnsWithException.filter { it.overdueStatus == OverdueStatus.EXCEPTED }
   249→            "not_excepted" -> vulnsWithException.filter { it.overdueStatus != OverdueStatus.EXCEPTED }
   250→            "overdue" -> vulnsWithException.filter { it.overdueStatus == OverdueStatus.OVERDUE }
   251→            "ok" -> vulnsWithException.filter { it.overdueStatus == OverdueStatus.OK }
   252→            else -> vulnsWithException
   253→        }
   254→
   255→        val totalElements = result.size.toLong()
   256→        
   257→        // Apply pagination
   258→        val startIndex = page * size
   259→        val endIndex = minOf(startIndex + size, result.size)
   260→        
   261→        val paginatedResult = if (startIndex < result.size) {
   262→            result.subList(startIndex, endIndex)
   263→        } else {
   264→            emptyList()
   265→        }
   266→
   267→        log.info("Returning {} current vulnerabilities (page {}, size {}), total: {}",
   268→            paginatedResult.size, page, size, totalElements)
   269→        return Pair(paginatedResult, totalElements)
   270→    }
   271→
   272→    /**
   273→     * PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZED: Get current vulnerabilities with proper database pagination
   274→     *
   275→     * This method uses database-level pagination and access control, replacing the
   276→     * inefficient pattern of loading all data and filtering in-memory.
   277→     *
   278→     * Key optimizations:
   279→     * 1. Database-level pagination (only fetches requested page)
   280→     * 2. Database-level access control (filters by asset IDs in SQL)
   281→     * 3. Cached active exceptions (5-minute TTL)
   282→     *
   283→     * Note: Exception status filtering still happens in-memory on the paginated result.
   284→     * This means the returned page may have fewer items than requested if filtered.
   285→     * For exact pagination with exception status, the caller should request larger pages
   286→     * or use the legacy getCurrentVulnerabilities method.
   287→     *
   288→     * Feature: Database Structure Optimization - Phase 2
   289→     *
   290→     * @param accessibleAssetIds Set of asset IDs the user can access (from AssetFilterService)
   291→     * @param isAdmin If true, bypasses asset ID filtering (ADMIN has full access)
   292→     * @param severity Optional CVSS severity filter
   293→     * @param system Optional system/asset name filter
   294→     * @param exceptionStatus Optional filter: "excepted", "not_excepted", "overdue", "ok", or null
   295→     * @param product Optional product/version filter (substring match)
   296→     * @param adDomain Optional AD domain filter (substring match)
   297→     * @param cloudAccountId Optional AWS cloud account ID filter (substring match)
   298→     * @param page Page number (0-indexed)
   299→     * @param size Page size

    38→/**
    39→ * Controller for vulnerability management and exception operations
    40→ *
    41→ * Endpoints:
    42→ * - GET /api/vulnerabilities/current - List current vulnerabilities with filters
    43→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exceptions - List vulnerability exceptions
    44→ * - POST /api/vulnerability-exceptions - Create new exception
    45→ * - PUT /api/vulnerability-exceptions/{id} - Update exception
    46→ * - DELETE /api/vulnerability-exceptions/{id} - Delete exception
    47→ *
    48→ * Access: ADMIN, VULN, and SECCHAMPION roles only
    49→ * Feature: 025-role-based-access-control
    50→ *
    51→ * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
    52→ */
    53→@Controller
    54→@Secured("ADMIN", "VULN", "SECCHAMPION")
    55→@ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.BLOCKING)
    56→open class VulnerabilityManagementController(
    57→    private val vulnerabilityService: VulnerabilityService,
    58→    private val vulnerabilityExceptionService: VulnerabilityExceptionService,
    59→    private val assetFilterService: AssetFilterService,
    60→    private val vulnerabilityTimestampMigrationService: com.secman.service.VulnerabilityTimestampMigrationService,
    61→    private val vulnerabilityExportService: VulnerabilityExportService,
    62→    private val exportJobService: ExportJobService
    63→) {
    64→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityManagementController::class.java)
    65→
    66→    @Serdeable
    67→    data class ErrorResponse(val error: String)
    68→
    69→    /**
    70→     * Get current vulnerabilities (latest scan per asset) with exception status
    71→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
    72→     *
    73→     * GET /api/vulnerabilities/current?severity=Critical&system=server&exceptionStatus=not_excepted&product=zlib&adDomain=CONTOSO&cloudAccountId=123456789012&page=0&size=50
    74→     * Auth: ADMIN or VULN role
    75→     * Response: PaginatedVulnerabilitiesResponse
    76→     *
    77→     * FR-014, FR-016, FR-018: ADMIN sees all, VULN role respects workgroup restrictions
    78→     *
    79→     * Query Parameters:
    80→     * - severity: Optional CVSS severity filter (e.g., "Critical", "High", "Medium", "Low")
    81→     * - system: Optional system/asset name filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
    82→     * - exceptionStatus: Optional filter - "excepted", "not_excepted", or omit for all
    83→     * - product: Optional product/version filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
    84→     * - adDomain: Optional AD domain filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
    85→     * - cloudAccountId: Optional AWS cloud account ID filter (substring match, case-insensitive)
    86→     * - page: Page number (0-indexed, default: 0)
    87→     * - size: Page size (default: 50, max: 500)
    88→     * - sort: Sort field (e.g., "assetName", "cvssSeverity", "scanTimestamp", "vulnerabilityId", "vulnerableProductVersions", "daysOpen")
    89→     * - sortDir: Sort direction ("asc" or "desc", default: "desc")
    90→     */
    91→    @Get("/api/vulnerabilities/current")
    92→    @Secured("ADMIN", "VULN", "SECCHAMPION")
    93→    open fun getCurrentVulnerabilities(
    94→        authentication: Authentication,
    95→        @Nullable @QueryValue severity: String?,
    96→        @Nullable @QueryValue system: String?,
    97→        @Nullable @QueryValue exceptionStatus: String?,
    98→        @Nullable @QueryValue product: String?,
    99→        @Nullable @QueryValue adDomain: String?,
   100→        @Nullable @QueryValue cloudAccountId: String?,
   101→        @Nullable @QueryValue page: Int?,
   102→        @Nullable @QueryValue size: Int?,
   103→        @Nullable @QueryValue sort: String?,
   104→        @Nullable @QueryValue sortDir: String?
   105→    ): HttpResponse<PaginatedVulnerabilitiesResponse> {
   106→        return try {
   107→            // Validate and sanitize pagination parameters
   108→            val pageNumber = maxOf(page ?: 0, 0)
   109→            val pageSize = minOf(maxOf(size ?: 50, 1), 500)
   110→
   111→            // Default to excluding excepted vulnerabilities when no filter specified
   112→            val effectiveExceptionStatus = if (exceptionStatus.isNullOrBlank()) "not_excepted" else exceptionStatus
   113→
   114→            log.debug("Getting current vulnerabilities for user: {} - severity: {}, system: {}, exceptionStatus: {} (effective: {}), product: {}, adDomain: {}, cloudAccountId: {}, page: {}, size: {}",
   115→                authentication.name, severity, system, exceptionStatus, effectiveExceptionStatus, product, adDomain, cloudAccountId, pageNumber, pageSize)
   116→
   117→            // Check if user is ADMIN (universal access)
   118→            val isAdmin = authentication.roles.contains("ADMIN")
   119→
   120→            // Get accessible asset IDs (only needed for non-ADMIN users)
   121→            // PERFORMANCE: For ADMIN users, we skip this query entirely
   122→            val accessibleAssetIds = if (isAdmin) {
   123→                emptySet()  // Not used for ADMIN
   124→            } else {
   125→                assetFilterService.getAccessibleAssets(authentication)
   126→                    .mapNotNull { it.id }
   127→                    .toSet()
   128→            }
   129→
   130→            // PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZED: Use database-level pagination and access control
   131→            // This replaces the old pattern of loading all data and filtering in-memory
   132→            val response = vulnerabilityService.getCurrentVulnerabilitiesOptimized(
   133→                accessibleAssetIds = accessibleAssetIds,
   134→                isAdmin = isAdmin,
   135→                severity = severity,
   136→                system = system,
   137→                exceptionStatus = effectiveExceptionStatus,
   138→                product = product,
   139→                adDomain = adDomain,
   140→                cloudAccountId = cloudAccountId,
   141→                page = pageNumber,
   142→                size = pageSize,
   143→                sort = sort,
   144→                sortDir = sortDir
   145→            )
   146→
   147→            log.info("Returning {} current vulnerabilities for user {} (page {}/{}, total {})",
   148→                response.content.size, authentication.name, response.currentPage + 1, response.totalPages, response.totalElements)
   149→            HttpResponse.ok(response)
   150→

    18→/**
    19→ * Service for accessing outdated assets with workgroup-based access control
    20→ *
    21→ * Responsibilities:
    22→ * - Query materialized view with pagination, sorting, filtering
    23→ * - Apply workgroup-based access control (ADMIN sees all, VULN sees assigned workgroups only)
    24→ * - Support search and severity filtering
    25→ *
    26→ * Feature: 034-outdated-assets
    27→ * Task: T016-T019
    28→ * User Story: US1 - View Outdated Assets (P1)
    29→ * Spec reference: spec.md FR-008, FR-009
    30→ */
    31→@Singleton
    32→class OutdatedAssetService(
    33→    private val outdatedAssetRepository: OutdatedAssetMaterializedViewRepository,
    34→    private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository,
    35→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository
    36→) {
    37→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(OutdatedAssetService::class.java)
    38→
    39→    /**
    40→     * Get outdated assets with workgroup-based access control
    41→     *
    42→     * Access Control:
    43→     * - ADMIN role: sees all outdated assets (no filtering)
    44→     * - VULN role: sees only assets from assigned workgroups
    45→     * - No VULN/ADMIN: unauthorized (handled by controller @Secured)
    46→     *
    47→     * @param authentication Current user authentication context
    48→     * @param searchTerm Optional search term for asset name (case-insensitive)
    49→     * @param minSeverity Optional minimum severity filter (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW)
    50→     * @param adDomain Optional AD domain filter (case-insensitive exact match)
    51→     * @param pageable Pagination and sorting parameters
    52→     * @return Page of outdated assets visible to the user
    53→     */
    54→    fun getOutdatedAssets(
    55→        authentication: Authentication,
    56→        searchTerm: String? = null,
    57→        minSeverity: String? = null,
    58→        adDomain: String? = null,
    59→        pageable: Pageable
    60→    ): Page<OutdatedAssetMaterializedView> {
    61→        // Extract user's workgroup IDs from authentication attributes
    62→        val workgroupIds = extractWorkgroupIds(authentication)
    63→
    64→        // Check if user has ADMIN role - if so, they see everything
    65→        val isAdmin = authentication.roles.contains("ADMIN")
    66→
    67→        // Build workgroup filter parameter
    68→        val workgroupFilter = if (isAdmin) {
    69→            // ADMIN sees all - pass null to disable workgroup filtering
    70→            null
    71→        } else {
    72→            // VULN user - filter by their assigned workgroups
    73→            // Convert list of IDs to comma-separated string for LIKE query
    74→            workgroupIds?.joinToString(",")
    75→        }
    76→
    77→        // Query repository with filters
    78→        // Create pageable without sorting to avoid JPQL query issues
    79→        val unsortedPageable = Pageable.from(pageable.number, pageable.size)
    80→
    81→        return outdatedAssetRepository.findOutdatedAssets(
    82→            workgroupId = workgroupFilter,
    83→            searchTerm = searchTerm,
    84→            minSeverity = minSeverity,
    85→            adDomain = adDomain,
    86→            pageable = unsortedPageable
    87→        )
    88→    }
    89→
    90→    /**
    91→     * Extract workgroup IDs from authentication context
    92→     *
    93→     * Workgroup IDs are stored in authentication attributes as "workgroupIds"
    94→     * Expected format: List<Long>
    95→     *
    96→     * @param authentication User authentication context
    97→     * @return List of workgroup IDs or null if not present
    98→     */
    99→    private fun extractWorkgroupIds(authentication: Authentication): List<Long>? {
   100→        val workgroupIdsAttr = authentication.attributes["workgroupIds"] ?: return null
   101→
   102→        return when (workgroupIdsAttr) {
   103→            is List<*> -> workgroupIdsAttr.filterIsInstance<Long>()
   104→            is Collection<*> -> workgroupIdsAttr.filterIsInstance<Long>()
   105→            else -> null
   106→        }
   107→    }
   108→
   109→    /**
   110→     * Get the latest refresh timestamp
   111→     *
   112→     * @return Latest timestamp from materialized view, or null if no data
   113→     */
   114→    fun getLastRefreshTimestamp(): java.time.LocalDateTime? {
   115→        return outdatedAssetRepository.findLatestCalculatedAt()
   116→    }
   117→
   118→    /**
   119→     * Get distinct AD domains for filter dropdown
   120→     *
   121→     * Queries the Asset table directly to get all AD domains in the system,
   122→     * rather than the materialized view which may not be refreshed.
   123→     *
   124→     * @return List of unique AD domain values, ordered alphabetically
   125→     */
   126→    fun getDistinctAdDomains(): List<String> {
   127→        return assetRepository.findDistinctAdDomains()
   128→    }
   129→
   130→    /**
   131→     * Count total outdated assets visible to user
   132→     *
   133→     * @param authentication Current user authentication context
   134→     * @return Total count respecting workgroup access control
   135→     */
   136→    fun countOutdatedAssets(authentication: Authentication): Long {
   137→        val isAdmin = authentication.roles.contains("ADMIN")
   138→
   139→        return if (isAdmin) {
   140→            outdatedAssetRepository.count()
   141→        } else {
   142→            val workgroupIds = extractWorkgroupIds(authentication)
   143→            val workgroupFilter = workgroupIds?.joinToString(",")
   144→
   145→            outdatedAssetRepository.countOutdatedAssets(
   146→                workgroupId = workgroupFilter
   147→            )
   148→        }
   149→    }
   150→
   151→    /**
   152→     * Get single outdated asset by ID with access control
   153→     *
   154→     * Task: T032-T034
   155→     * User Story: US2 - View Asset Details
   156→     *
   157→     * @param id Outdated asset materialized view ID
   158→     * @param authentication Current user authentication context
   159→     * @return Outdated asset or null if not found or unauthorized
   160→     */
   161→    fun getOutdatedAssetById(
   162→        id: Long,
   163→        authentication: Authentication
   164→    ): OutdatedAssetMaterializedView? {
   165→        val asset = outdatedAssetRepository.findById(id).orElse(null) ?: return null
   166→
   167→        // Check access control
   168→        val isAdmin = authentication.roles.contains("ADMIN")
   169→        if (isAdmin) {
   170→            // ADMIN sees all
   171→            return asset
   172→        }
   173→
   174→        // VULN user - check workgroup access
   175→        val userWorkgroupIds = extractWorkgroupIds(authentication) ?: emptyList()
   176→
   177→        // If asset has no workgroups, allow access (backward compatibility)
   178→        if (asset.workgroupIds.isNullOrBlank()) {
   179→            return asset
   180→        }
   181→
   182→        // Parse asset's workgroup IDs
   183→        val workgroupIdsStr = asset.workgroupIds ?: ""
   184→        val assetWorkgroupIds = workgroupIdsStr.split(",").mapNotNull { it.toLongOrNull() }
   185→
   186→        // Check if user has access to any of the asset's workgroups
   187→        val hasAccess = assetWorkgroupIds.any { it in userWorkgroupIds }
   188→
   189→        return if (hasAccess) asset else null
   190→    }
   191→
   192→    /**
   193→     * Get vulnerabilities for an outdated asset
   194→     *
   195→     * Task: T037-T038
   196→     * User Story: US2 - View Asset Details
   197→     *
   198→     * @param assetId The actual asset ID (not materialized view ID)
   199→     * @param pageable Pagination parameters
   200→     * @return Page of vulnerabilities for the asset
   201→     */
   202→    fun getVulnerabilitiesForAsset(
   203→        assetId: Long,
   204→        pageable: Pageable
   205→    ): Page<Vulnerability> {
   206→        return vulnerabilityRepository.findByAssetId(assetId, pageable)
   207→    }
   208→
   209→    /**
   210→     * Export outdated assets to Excel with the same filters as the list view
   211→     *
   212→     * @param authentication Current user authentication context
   213→     * @param searchTerm Optional search term for asset name
   214→     * @param minSeverity Optional minimum severity filter
   215→     * @param adDomain Optional AD domain filter
   216→     * @return ByteArrayOutputStream containing the Excel workbook
   217→     */
   218→    fun exportOutdatedAssets(
   219→        authentication: Authentication,
   220→        searchTerm: String? = null,
   221→        minSeverity: String? = null,
   222→        adDomain: String? = null
   223→    ): ByteArrayOutputStream {
   224→        // Fetch all matching assets (large page to get everything)
   225→        val page = getOutdatedAssets(
   226→            authentication = authentication,
   227→            searchTerm = searchTerm,
   228→            minSeverity = minSeverity,
   229→            adDomain = adDomain,
   230→            pageable = Pageable.from(0, 100_000)
   231→        )
   232→        val assets = page.content
   233→
   234→        log.info("Exporting {} outdated assets to Excel for user: {}", assets.size, authentication.name)
   235→
   236→        val workbook = SXSSFWorkbook(100)
   237→        workbook.setCompressTempFiles(true)
   238→
   239→        try {
   240→            val sheet = workbook.createSheet("Outdated Assets")
   241→
   242→            // Header style
   243→            val headerStyle = workbook.createCellStyle().apply {
   244→                fillForegroundColor = IndexedColors.GREY_25_PERCENT.index
   245→                fillPattern = FillPatternType.SOLID_FOREGROUND
   246→                val font = workbook.createFont()
   247→                font.bold = true
   248→                setFont(font)
   249→            }
   250→
   251→            // Header row
   252→            val headerRow = sheet.createRow(0)
   253→            val headers = listOf(
   254→                "Asset Name", "Asset Type", "AD Domain",
   255→                "Total Overdue", "Critical", "High", "Medium", "Low",
   256→                "Oldest Vuln (Days)", "Oldest Vuln ID"
   257→            )
   258→            headers.forEachIndexed { index, header ->
   259→                headerRow.createCell(index).apply {
   260→                    setCellValue(header)
   261→                    cellStyle = headerStyle
   262→                }
   263→            }
   264→
   265→            // Data rows
   266→            assets.forEachIndexed { index, asset ->
   267→                val row = sheet.createRow(index + 1)
   268→                row.createCell(0).setCellValue(asset.assetName)
   269→                row.createCell(1).setCellValue(asset.assetType)
   270→                row.createCell(2).setCellValue(asset.adDomain ?: "")
   271→                row.createCell(3).setCellValue(asset.totalOverdueCount.toDouble())
   272→                row.createCell(4).setCellValue(asset.criticalCount.toDouble())
   273→                row.createCell(5).setCellValue(asset.highCount.toDouble())
   274→                row.createCell(6).setCellValue(asset.mediumCount.toDouble())
   275→                row.createCell(7).setCellValue(asset.lowCount.toDouble())
   276→                row.createCell(8).setCellValue(asset.oldestVulnDays.toDouble())
   277→                row.createCell(9).setCellValue(asset.oldestVulnId ?: "")
   278→            }
   279→
   280→            // Column widths
   281→            sheet.setColumnWidth(0, 40 * 256)  // Asset Name
   282→            sheet.setColumnWidth(1, 15 * 256)  // Asset Type
   283→            sheet.setColumnWidth(2, 25 * 256)  // AD Domain
   284→            sheet.setColumnWidth(3, 15 * 256)  // Total Overdue
   285→            sheet.setColumnWidth(4, 12 * 256)  // Critical
   286→            sheet.setColumnWidth(5, 12 * 256)  // High
   287→            sheet.setColumnWidth(6, 12 * 256)  // Medium
   288→            sheet.setColumnWidth(7, 12 * 256)  // Low
   289→            sheet.setColumnWidth(8, 18 * 256)  // Oldest Vuln Days
   290→            sheet.setColumnWidth(9, 20 * 256)  // Oldest Vuln ID
   291→
   292→            val outputStream = ByteArrayOutputStream()
   293→            workbook.write(outputStream)
   294→            return outputStream
   295→        } finally {
   296→            @Suppress("DEPRECATION") workbook.dispose()
   297→        }
   298→    }
   299→}

    18→/**
    19→ * Query command for CrowdStrike vulnerabilities
    20→ *
    21→ * Usage:
    22→ *   secman query --hostname <hostname> [options]
    23→ *
    24→ * Functionality:
    25→ * - Query CrowdStrike Falcon API for vulnerabilities
    26→ * - Filter by severity and product
    27→ * - Support pagination
    28→ * - Export to JSON or CSV
    29→ * - Optionally save to database (--save flag, direct DB access)
    30→ *
    31→ * Related to: Feature 023-create-in-the, 032-servers-query-import, 073-cli-direct-db
    32→ */
    33→class QueryCommand {
    34→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(QueryCommand::class.java)
    35→    private val configLoader = ConfigLoader()
    36→    private val exportService = ExportService()
    37→    private val appContext = ApplicationContext.run()
    38→    private val apiClient: CrowdStrikeApiClient = appContext.getBean(CrowdStrikeApiClient::class.java)
    39→    private val importService: CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService = appContext.getBean(CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService::class.java)
    40→
    41→    var hostname: String = ""
    42→    var outputPath: String? = null
    43→    var outputFile: String? = null
    44→    var format: String = "json"
    45→    var severity: String? = null
    46→    var product: String? = null
    47→    var limit: Int = 100
    48→    var clientId: String? = null
    49→    var clientSecret: String? = null
    50→    var verbose: Boolean = false
    51→    var save: Boolean = false
    52→
    53→    fun execute(): Int {
    54→        return try {
    55→            log.info("Querying vulnerabilities for hostname: {}", hostname)
    56→
    57→            // Validate input
    58→            if (hostname.isBlank()) {
    59→                System.err.println("Error: Hostname cannot be blank")
    60→                return 1
    61→            }
    62→
    63→            // Load or override configuration
    64→            val config = if (clientId != null && clientSecret != null) {
    65→                FalconConfigDto(
    66→                    clientId = clientId!!,
    67→                    clientSecret = clientSecret!!
    68→                )
    69→            } else {
    70→                configLoader.loadConfig()
    71→            }
    72→
    73→            log.info("Configuration loaded successfully")
    74→
    75→            // Query CrowdStrike API
    76→            log.info("Querying CrowdStrike API for hostname: {}", hostname)
    77→            System.out.println("Querying vulnerabilities for: $hostname")
    78→
    79→            val response = apiClient.queryAllVulnerabilities(hostname, config, limit)
    80→
    81→            // Filter by severity if specified (supports comma-separated values)
    82→            val filteredByServerity = if (severity != null) {
    83→                val severityLevels = severity!!.split(",").map { it.trim().lowercase() }
    84→                if (verbose) {
    85→                    System.out.println("Filtering by severity: ${severityLevels.joinToString(", ")}")
    86→                }
    87→                response.copy(
    88→                    vulnerabilities = response.vulnerabilities.filter {
    89→                        severityLevels.contains(it.severity.lowercase())
    90→                    }
    91→                )
    92→            } else {
    93→                response
    94→            }
    95→
    96→            // Filter by product if specified
    97→            val filteredByProduct = if (product != null) {
    98→                filteredByServerity.copy(
    99→                    vulnerabilities = filteredByServerity.vulnerabilities.filter {
   100→                        it.affectedProduct?.contains(product!!, ignoreCase = true) == true
   101→                    }
   102→                )
   103→            } else {
   104→                filteredByServerity
   105→            }
   106→
   107→            // Apply final filters
   108→            val finalResponse = filteredByProduct
   109→
   110→            System.out.println("Total vulnerabilities found: ${finalResponse.vulnerabilities.size}")
   111→
   112→            // Show severity breakdown if verbose
   113→            if (verbose && finalResponse.vulnerabilities.isNotEmpty()) {
   114→                val severityCount = finalResponse.vulnerabilities
   115→                    .groupBy { it.severity }
   116→                    .mapValues { it.value.size }
   117→                    .toList()
   118→                    .sortedByDescending { it.second }
   119→
   120→                System.out.println("\nSeverity breakdown:")
   121→                severityCount.forEach { (sev, count) ->
   122→                    System.out.println("  - $sev: $count")
   123→                }
   124→            }
   125→
   126→            // Save to database if --save flag is specified
   127→            if (save && finalResponse.vulnerabilities.isNotEmpty()) {
   128→                System.out.println("\nSaving to database...")
   129→
   130→                // Build a single batch DTO from the hostname + filtered vulnerabilities
   131→                val firstVuln = finalResponse.vulnerabilities.firstOrNull()
   132→                val batch = CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto(
   133→                    hostname = hostname,
   134→                    groups = null,
   135→                    cloudAccountId = firstVuln?.cloudAccountId,
   136→                    cloudInstanceId = firstVuln?.cloudInstanceId,
   137→                    adDomain = firstVuln?.adDomain,
   138→                    osVersion = null,
   139→                    ip = firstVuln?.ip,
   140→                    vulnerabilities = finalResponse.vulnerabilities.map { vuln ->
   141→                        VulnerabilityDto(
   142→                            cveId = vuln.cveId ?: "",
   143→                            severity = vuln.severity,
   144→                            affectedProduct = vuln.affectedProduct,
   145→                            daysOpen = parseDaysOpenToInt(vuln.daysOpen),
   146→                            patchPublicationDate = vuln.patchPublicationDate
   147→                        )
   148→                    }
   149→                )
   150→
   151→                val result = importService.importServerVulnerabilities(listOf(batch), "CLI")
   152→
   153→                System.out.println("Save completed!")
   154→                System.out.println("  - Asset: ${if (result.serversCreated > 0) "CREATED" else "UPDATED"}")
   155→                System.out.println("  - Vulnerabilities imported: ${result.vulnerabilitiesImported}")
   156→                System.out.println("  - Vulnerabilities skipped: ${result.vulnerabilitiesSkipped}")
   157→
   158→                if (result.errors.isNotEmpty()) {
   159→                    System.err.println("  - Errors:")
   160→                    result.errors.forEach { error ->
   161→                        System.err.println("    - $error")
   162→                    }
   163→                }
   164→            } else if (save && finalResponse.vulnerabilities.isEmpty()) {
   165→                System.out.println("\nNo vulnerabilities to save")
   166→            }
   167→
   168→            // Export results if output file is specified
   169→            if (outputFile != null && finalResponse.vulnerabilities.isNotEmpty()) {
   170→                val outFile = File(outputFile!!)
   171→                val exported = when (format.lowercase()) {
   172→                    "csv" -> {
   173→                        exportService.exportToCsv(finalResponse, outFile)
   174→                    }
   175→                    else -> {
   176→                        exportService.exportToJson(finalResponse, outFile)
   177→                    }
   178→                }
   179→
   180→                if (exported) {
   181→                    System.out.println("Results exported to ${format.uppercase()}: ${outFile.absolutePath}")
   182→                } else {
   183→                    System.out.println("Export cancelled by user")
   184→                }
   185→            }
   186→
   187→            0
   188→        } catch (e: NotFoundException) {
   189→            System.err.println()
   190→            System.err.println("Error: Hostname '$hostname' not found in CrowdStrike")
   191→            System.err.println("   The hostname may not exist or may not be monitored by CrowdStrike Falcon.")
   192→            System.err.println("   Please verify the hostname is correct and the device is enrolled.")
   193→            1
   194→        } catch (e: AuthenticationException) {
   195→            System.err.println()
   196→            System.err.println("Error: CrowdStrike authentication failed")
   197→            System.err.println("   Please check your CrowdStrike API credentials.")
   198→            System.err.println("   Run 'secman config --show' to verify your configuration.")
   199→            1
   200→        } catch (e: RateLimitException) {
   201→            System.err.println()
   202→            System.err.println("Error: CrowdStrike API rate limit exceeded")
   203→            System.err.println("   Please wait a few minutes before trying again.")
   204→            1
   205→        } catch (e: CrowdStrikeException) {
   206→            System.err.println()
   207→            System.err.println("Error: CrowdStrike API error")
   208→            System.err.println("   ${e.message}")
   209→            if (verbose) {
   210→                e.printStackTrace()
   211→            }
   212→            1
   213→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   214→            System.err.println("Error: ${e.message}")
   215→            1
   216→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   217→            System.err.println("Error: ${e.message}")
   218→            1
   219→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   220→            System.err.println("Error: ${e.message}")
   221→            if (verbose) {
   222→                e.printStackTrace()
   223→            }
   224→            1
   225→        }
   226→    }
   227→
   228→    /**
   229→     * Parse daysOpen string to integer value
   230→     *
   231→     * Handles formats: "526 days" -> 526, "1 day" -> 1, null -> 0
   232→     */
   233→    private fun parseDaysOpenToInt(daysOpen: String?): Int {
   234→        if (daysOpen.isNullOrBlank()) return 0
   235→        return daysOpen.split(" ").firstOrNull()?.toIntOrNull() ?: 0
   236→    }
   237→}
   238→

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.config.VulnerabilitySettings
     4→import com.secman.domain.Asset
     5→import com.secman.domain.CrowdStrikeImportHistory
     6→import com.secman.domain.Vulnerability
     7→import com.secman.dto.CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto
     8→import com.secman.dto.ImportStatisticsDto
     9→import com.secman.dto.CrowdStrikeImportStatusDto
    10→import com.secman.dto.VulnerabilityDto
    11→import com.secman.repository.AssetRepository
    12→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityRepository
    13→import com.secman.repository.CrowdStrikeImportHistoryRepository
    14→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    15→import jakarta.persistence.EntityManager
    16→import jakarta.persistence.LockModeType
    17→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
    18→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    19→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    20→import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit
    21→
    22→/**
    23→ * Service for importing CrowdStrike server vulnerabilities with automatic asset creation
    24→ *
    25→ * Provides functionality to:
    26→ * - Find or create Asset records from CrowdStrike server data
    27→ * - Import vulnerabilities with transactional replace pattern (delete old + insert new)
    28→ * - Track import statistics (servers created/updated, vulnerabilities imported/skipped)
    29→ * - Handle per-server transactions with rollback on failure
    30→ * - Preserve existing asset metadata (owner, description, workgroups) when reusing assets
    31→ *
    32→ * Feature: 032-servers-query-import
    33→ * Tasks: T019, T020, T021, T022
    34→ * Spec reference: FR-008, FR-010, FR-011, FR-012, FR-013, FR-015
    35→ */
    36→@Singleton
    37→open class CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService(
    38→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    39→    private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository,
    40→    private val importHistoryRepository: CrowdStrikeImportHistoryRepository,
    41→    private val materializedViewRefreshService: MaterializedViewRefreshService,
    42→    private val vulnerabilitySettings: VulnerabilitySettings,
    43→    private val entityManager: EntityManager
    44→) {
    45→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService::class.java)
    46→
    47→    /**
    48→     * Provider for self-reference to ensure @Transactional works on internal method calls.
    49→     * Using Provider<T> avoids circular dependency during bean construction.
    50→     * Feature 053: Fix AOP proxy bypass for transactional delete-insert pattern
    51→     */
    52→    @jakarta.inject.Inject
    53→    private lateinit var selfProvider: jakarta.inject.Provider<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService>
    54→
    55→    /**
    56→     * Import batch of server vulnerabilities from CrowdStrike
    57→     *
    58→     * Task: T022
    59→     * Spec reference: FR-008
    60→     *
    61→     * @param batches List of server vulnerability batches
    62→     * @param triggeredBy User/system that triggered the import (for audit trail)
    63→     * @param triggerRefresh Whether to trigger materialized view refresh after import.
    64→     *                       Set to false when importing multiple batches to avoid redundant refreshes.
    65→     *                       The caller should trigger a single refresh after all batches complete.
    66→     * @return ImportStatisticsDto with detailed statistics
    67→     */
    68→    fun importServerVulnerabilities(
    69→        batches: List<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto>,
    70→        triggeredBy: String? = null,
    71→        triggerRefresh: Boolean = true
    72→    ): ImportStatisticsDto {
    73→        log.info("Starting batch import of {} servers", batches.size)
    74→
    75→        var serversCreated = 0
    76→        var serversUpdated = 0
    77→        var vulnerabilitiesImported = 0
    78→        var vulnerabilitiesSkipped = 0
    79→        var vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate = 0
    80→        val uniqueDomains = mutableSetOf<String>()  // Feature 043: Track unique domains
    81→        val errors = mutableListOf<String>()
    82→
    83→        for (batch in batches) {
    84→            try {
    85→                // Process each server in its own transaction (FR-010)
    86→                // Use selfProvider.get() to ensure @Transactional is applied via AOP proxy (Feature 053)
    87→                val result = selfProvider.get().importVulnerabilitiesForServer(batch)
    88→
    89→                if (result.assetCreated) serversCreated++
    90→                else serversUpdated++
    91→
    92→                vulnerabilitiesImported += result.vulnerabilitiesImported
    93→                vulnerabilitiesSkipped += result.vulnerabilitiesSkipped
    94→                vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate += result.vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate
    95→
    96→                // Track unique domains (Feature 043)
    97→                batch.adDomain?.let { domain ->
    98→                    if (domain.isNotBlank()) {
    99→                        uniqueDomains.add(domain.trim().uppercase())
   100→                        log.trace("Tracked domain: {}", domain.trim().uppercase())
   101→                    }
   102→                }
   103→
   104→                log.debug("Imported server '{}': created={}, imported={}, skipped={}, withPatchDate={}, domain={}",
   105→                    batch.hostname, result.assetCreated, result.vulnerabilitiesImported, result.vulnerabilitiesSkipped, result.vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate, batch.adDomain)
   106→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   107→                val errorMsg = "Failed to import server '${batch.hostname}': ${e.message}"
   108→                log.error(errorMsg, e)
   109→                errors.add(errorMsg)
   110→            }
   111→        }
   112→
   113→        val statistics = ImportStatisticsDto(
   114→            serversProcessed = batches.size,
   115→            serversCreated = serversCreated,
   116→            serversUpdated = serversUpdated,
   117→            vulnerabilitiesImported = vulnerabilitiesImported,
   118→            vulnerabilitiesSkipped = vulnerabilitiesSkipped,
   119→            vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate = vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate,
   120→            uniqueDomainCount = uniqueDomains.size,  // Feature 043
   121→            discoveredDomains = uniqueDomains.sorted(),  // Feature 043
   122→            errors = errors
   123→        )
   124→
   125→        log.info("Batch import completed: processed={}, created={}, updated={}, imported={}, skipped={}, withPatchDate={}, uniqueDomains={}, errors={}",
   126→            batches.size, serversCreated, serversUpdated, vulnerabilitiesImported, vulnerabilitiesSkipped, vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate, uniqueDomains.size, errors.size)
   127→
   128→        recordImportHistory(statistics, triggeredBy)
   129→
   130→        // Trigger async materialized view refresh after import (if requested)
   131→        // Feature: 034-outdated-assets, Task: T101
   132→        // Spec reference: FR-005 (async refresh)
   133→        // Note: When importing multiple batches, set triggerRefresh=false to avoid redundant refreshes.
   134→        // The caller should trigger a single refresh after all batches complete.
   135→        if (triggerRefresh && vulnerabilitiesImported > 0) {
   136→            log.info("Triggering materialized view refresh after importing {} vulnerabilities", vulnerabilitiesImported)
   137→            materializedViewRefreshService.triggerAsyncRefresh("CLI Import - $vulnerabilitiesImported vulnerabilities imported")
   138→        } else if (!triggerRefresh && vulnerabilitiesImported > 0) {
   139→            log.debug("Skipping materialized view refresh (triggerRefresh=false, caller will handle)")
   140→        }
   141→
   142→        return statistics
   143→    }
   144→
   145→    /**
   146→     * Persist import execution metadata for UI consumption.
   147→     */
   148→    private fun recordImportHistory(statistics: ImportStatisticsDto, triggeredBy: String?) {
   149→        try {
   150→            val history = CrowdStrikeImportHistory(
   151→                importedAt = LocalDateTime.now(),
   152→                importedBy = triggeredBy,
   153→                serversProcessed = statistics.serversProcessed,
   154→                serversCreated = statistics.serversCreated,
   155→                serversUpdated = statistics.serversUpdated,
   156→                vulnerabilitiesImported = statistics.vulnerabilitiesImported,
   157→                vulnerabilitiesSkipped = statistics.vulnerabilitiesSkipped,
   158→                vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate = statistics.vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate,
   159→                errorCount = statistics.errors.size
   160→            )
   161→            importHistoryRepository.save(history)
   162→            log.info("Recorded CrowdStrike import history entry: {}", history)
   163→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   164→            log.error("Failed to record CrowdStrike import history", e)
   165→        }
   166→    }
   167→
   168→    fun getLatestImportStatus(): CrowdStrikeImportStatusDto? {
   169→        return importHistoryRepository.findLatest()
   170→            ?.let { CrowdStrikeImportStatusDto.fromEntity(it) }
   171→    }
   172→
   173→    /**
   174→     * Import vulnerabilities for a single server with transactional replace pattern
   175→     *
   176→     * **Duplicate Prevention Strategy (Feature 048):**
   177→     * This method prevents duplicate vulnerability entries by implementing a transactional replace pattern:
   178→     * 1. DELETE all existing vulnerabilities for the asset
   179→     * 2. INSERT new vulnerabilities from the import batch
   180→     * 3. All operations wrapped in @Transactional boundary
   181→     *
   182→     * This approach ensures:
   183→     * - **Idempotency**: Running the same import multiple times produces identical results
   184→     * - **No duplicates**: Each (asset, CVE) combination exists exactly once
   185→     * - **Atomicity**: Either all vulnerabilities are replaced, or none are (on failure)
   186→     * - **Remediation tracking**: Removed CVEs indicate vulnerabilities have been patched
   187→     *
   188→     * The transactional replace pattern is preferred over upsert because:
   189→     * - Simpler logic: No need to detect which records to update vs insert
   190→     * - Accurate remediation: Missing CVEs in new import = vulnerability fixed
   191→     * - Better performance: Bulk delete + bulk insert faster than individual upserts
   192→     * - Cleaner state: No orphaned records from complex merge logic
   193→     *
   194→     * **Important Implementation Detail:**
   195→     * The Asset.vulnerabilities relationship MUST NOT use `cascade = [CascadeType.ALL]` or `orphanRemoval = true`.
   196→     * Manual delete-insert operations conflict with JPA cascade behavior, causing newly inserted
   197→     * vulnerabilities to be cascade-deleted when the transaction commits. This service performs
   198→     * explicit vulnerability deletion via `vulnerabilityRepository.deleteByAssetId()` before insertion.
   199→     *
   200→     * Tasks: T021, T024, T025, T026
   201→     * Spec reference: FR-010, FR-011, Feature 048
   202→     *
   203→     * @param batch Server vulnerability batch containing hostname and vulnerabilities
   204→     * @return ServerImportResult with statistics (vulnerabilities imported/skipped, asset created/updated)
   205→     */
   206→    @Transactional
   207→    open fun importVulnerabilitiesForServer(batch: CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto): ServerImportResult {
   208→        log.debug("Importing vulnerabilities for server: {}", batch.hostname)
   209→
   210→        // Feature 053: Duplicate Asset Cleanup
   211→        // Instead of strict exact match, we look for potential duplicates (FQDN vs Shortname)
   212→        val shortName = batch.hostname.substringBefore(".")
   213→        val candidates = assetRepository.findPotentialDuplicates(shortName)
   214→
   215→        val (asset, isNewAsset) = if (candidates.isNotEmpty()) {
   216→            // Pick best match: exact match first, or the one with content, or just the first one
   217→            val bestMatch = candidates.find { it.name.equals(batch.hostname, ignoreCase = true) } 
   218→                           ?: candidates.first()
   219→            
   220→            // Clean up: We will delete vulnerabilities for ALL candidates to avoid inflation
   221→            // The "best match" will get new vulnerabilities populated below
   222→            
   223→            Pair(updateAsset(bestMatch, batch), false)
   224→        } else {
   225→            Pair(createNewAsset(batch), true)
   226→        }
   227→
   228→        // Delete existing vulnerabilities for this asset AND any duplicates
   229→        // This ensures that "stale" duplicate assets don't contribute to inflated counts
   230→        val assetIdsToClean = if (candidates.isNotEmpty()) {
   231→            candidates.mapNotNull { it.id }
   232→        } else {
   233→            listOf(asset.id!!)
   234→        }
   235→
   236→        var deletedCount = 0
   237→        for (id in assetIdsToClean) {
   238→            // Lock asset to prevent concurrent import races
   239→            entityManager.find(Asset::class.java, id, LockModeType.PESSIMISTIC_WRITE)
   240→            val count = vulnerabilityRepository.deleteByAssetId(id)
   241→            deletedCount += count
   242→        }
   243→        
   244→        if (deletedCount > 0) {
   245→            log.debug("Deleted {} existing vulnerabilities for asset '{}' (and {} duplicates)", 
   246→                deletedCount, asset.name, assetIdsToClean.size - 1)
   247→        }
   248→
   249→        // Filter vulnerabilities: require CVE ID and optionally require patch publication date
   250→        // Combined into a single pass to reduce intermediate list allocations (memory optimization)
   251→        val requirePatchDate = vulnerabilitySettings.requirePatchPublicationDate
   252→        var vulnsWithCve = batch.vulnerabilities.filter { vuln ->
   253→            !vuln.cveId.isNullOrBlank() && (!requirePatchDate || vuln.patchPublicationDate != null)
   254→        }
   255→        val skippedCount = batch.vulnerabilities.size - vulnsWithCve.size
   256→
   257→        if (skippedCount > 0) {
   258→            log.debug("Skipped {} vulnerabilities (no CVE ID{}) for asset '{}'",
   259→                skippedCount, if (requirePatchDate) " or no patch publication date" else "", asset.name)
   260→        }
   261→
   262→        // Feature 054: Deduplicate by (CVE ID, affected product) before inserting
   263→        // CrowdStrike API may return true duplicates from overlapping pagination batches
   264→        // We keep the one with the highest daysOpen value (oldest occurrence)
   265→        // Different products for the same CVE are preserved as separate entries
   266→        val beforeDedup = vulnsWithCve.size
   267→        vulnsWithCve = vulnsWithCve
   268→            .groupBy { Pair(it.cveId, it.affectedProduct) }
   269→            .map { (_, duplicates) -> duplicates.maxByOrNull { it.daysOpen } ?: duplicates.first() }
   270→
   271→        val dedupCount = beforeDedup - vulnsWithCve.size
   272→        if (dedupCount > 0) {
   273→            log.info("Deduplicated {} duplicate (CVE, product) entries for asset '{}' ({} -> {} unique)",
   274→                dedupCount, asset.name, beforeDedup, vulnsWithCve.size)
   275→        }
   276→
   277→        // Create and save vulnerability records in chunks (T021)
   278→        // Entity creation is merged into the chunk loop so only chunkSize entities live at any time,
   279→        // rather than creating all entities upfront (which holds O(all_vulns) in memory).
   280→        val importTimestamp = LocalDateTime.now()
   281→        var vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDateCount = 0
   282→        var totalCreated = 0
   283→        val chunkSize = 500
   284→
   285→        if (vulnsWithCve.isNotEmpty()) {
   286→            val totalChunks = (vulnsWithCve.size + chunkSize - 1) / chunkSize
   287→            vulnsWithCve.chunked(chunkSize).forEachIndexed { index, dtoChunk ->
   288→                val entities = dtoChunk.map { vulnDto ->
   289→                    if (vulnDto.patchPublicationDate != null) {
   290→                        vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDateCount++
   291→                    }
   292→
   293→                    // Calculate scan timestamp and days open
   294→                    val (scanTimestamp, daysOpenText) = if (vulnerabilitySettings.usePatchPublicationDate && vulnDto.patchPublicationDate != null) {
   295→                        val patchDate = vulnDto.patchPublicationDate
   296→                        val daysFromPatch = ChronoUnit.DAYS.between(patchDate, importTimestamp).toInt()
   297→                        val daysText = if (daysFromPatch == 1) "1 day" else "$daysFromPatch days"
   298→                        log.trace("Using patch publication date for CVE {}: patch_date={}, days_from_patch={}",
   299→                            vulnDto.cveId, patchDate, daysFromPatch)
   300→                        Pair(patchDate, daysText)
   301→                    } else {
   302→                        val discoveryTimestamp = importTimestamp.minusDays(vulnDto.daysOpen.toLong())
   303→                        val daysText = "${vulnDto.daysOpen} days"
   304→                        Pair(discoveryTimestamp, daysText)
   305→                    }
   306→
   307→                    Vulnerability(
   308→                        asset = asset,
   309→                        vulnerabilityId = vulnDto.cveId,
   310→                        cvssSeverity = vulnDto.severity,
   311→                        vulnerableProductVersions = vulnDto.affectedProduct,
   312→                        daysOpen = daysOpenText,
   313→                        scanTimestamp = scanTimestamp,
   314→                        importTimestamp = importTimestamp,
   315→                        patchPublicationDate = vulnDto.patchPublicationDate
   316→                    )
   317→                }
   318→
   319→                vulnerabilityRepository.saveAll(entities)
   320→                totalCreated += entities.size
   321→
   322→                // Flush/clear for all but the last chunk to keep Hibernate L1 cache bounded
   323→                if (index < totalChunks - 1) {
   324→                    entityManager.flush()
   325→                    entityManager.clear()
   326→                    entityManager.merge(asset)
   327→                    log.trace("Saved vulnerability chunk {}/{} for asset '{}'",
   328→                        index + 1, totalChunks, asset.name)
   329→                }
   330→            }
   331→            log.debug("Created {} new vulnerabilities for asset '{}' ({} with patch publication date)",
   332→                totalCreated, asset.name, vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDateCount)
   333→        }
   334→
   335→        return ServerImportResult(
   336→            assetCreated = isNewAsset,
   337→            vulnerabilitiesImported = totalCreated,
   338→            vulnerabilitiesSkipped = skippedCount,
   339→            vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate = vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDateCount
   340→        )
   341→    }
   342→
   343→    private fun createNewAsset(batch: CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto): Asset {
   344→        val newAsset = Asset(
   345→            name = batch.hostname,
   346→            type = "SERVER",  // FR-002
   347→            owner = "CrowdStrike Import",  // FR-012
   348→            description = null,
   349→            ip = batch.ip?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   350→            groups = batch.groups?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   351→            cloudAccountId = batch.cloudAccountId?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   352→            cloudInstanceId = batch.cloudInstanceId?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   353→            adDomain = batch.adDomain?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   354→            osVersion = batch.osVersion?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   355→            lastSeen = LocalDateTime.now(),
   356→            manualCreator = null,  // Not manually created
   357→            scanUploader = null    // Not from scan upload
   358→        )
   359→
   360→        val created = assetRepository.save(newAsset)
   361→        log.debug("Created new asset: {} (id={})", batch.hostname, created.id)
   362→        return created
   363→    }
   364→
   365→    private fun updateAsset(existing: Asset, batch: CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto): Asset {
   366→        // Smart update: Only modify fields that have changed (Feature 043: US3)
   367→        var hasChanges = false
   368→        val changedFields = mutableListOf<String>()
   369→
   370→        // Compare and update IP
   371→        val newIp = batch.ip?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   372→        if (newIp != null && newIp != existing.ip) {
   373→            log.debug("IP changed for {}: {} -> {}", batch.hostname, existing.ip, newIp)
   374→            existing.ip = newIp
   375→            hasChanges = true
   376→            changedFields.add("ip")
   377→        }
   378→
   379→        // Compare and update groups
   380→        val newGroups = batch.groups?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   381→        if (newGroups != null && newGroups != existing.groups) {
   382→            log.debug("Groups changed for {}: {} -> {}", batch.hostname, existing.groups, newGroups)
   383→            existing.groups = newGroups
   384→            hasChanges = true
   385→            changedFields.add("groups")
   386→        }
   387→
   388→        // Compare and update cloud account ID
   389→        val newCloudAccountId = batch.cloudAccountId?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   390→        if (newCloudAccountId != null && newCloudAccountId != existing.cloudAccountId) {
   391→            log.debug("Cloud account ID changed for {}: {} -> {}", batch.hostname, existing.cloudAccountId, newCloudAccountId)
   392→            existing.cloudAccountId = newCloudAccountId
   393→            hasChanges = true
   394→            changedFields.add("cloudAccountId")
   395→        }
   396→
   397→        // Compare and update cloud instance ID
   398→        val newCloudInstanceId = batch.cloudInstanceId?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   399→        if (newCloudInstanceId != null && newCloudInstanceId != existing.cloudInstanceId) {
   400→            log.debug("Cloud instance ID changed for {}: {} -> {}", batch.hostname, existing.cloudInstanceId, newCloudInstanceId)
   401→            existing.cloudInstanceId = newCloudInstanceId
   402→            hasChanges = true
   403→            changedFields.add("cloudInstanceId")
   404→        }
   405→
   406→        // Compare and update AD domain (case-insensitive, normalized to lowercase by @PreUpdate)
   407→        val newAdDomain = batch.adDomain?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   408→        if (newAdDomain != null && newAdDomain.lowercase() != existing.adDomain?.lowercase()) {
   409→            log.debug("AD domain changed for {}: {} -> {}", batch.hostname, existing.adDomain, newAdDomain)
   410→            existing.adDomain = newAdDomain
   411→            hasChanges = true
   412→            changedFields.add("adDomain")
   413→        }
   414→
   415→        // Compare and update OS version
   416→        val newOsVersion = batch.osVersion?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   417→        if (newOsVersion != null && newOsVersion != existing.osVersion) {
   418→            log.debug("OS version changed for {}: {} -> {}", batch.hostname, existing.osVersion, newOsVersion)
   419→            existing.osVersion = newOsVersion
   420→            hasChanges = true
   421→            changedFields.add("osVersion")
   422→        }
   423→
   424→        // Always update lastSeen
   425→        existing.lastSeen = LocalDateTime.now()
   426→
   427→        // Only write to database if fields changed
   428→        return if (hasChanges) {
   429→            existing.updatedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   430→            assetRepository.update(existing)
   431→            log.info("Updated asset '{}' (id={}): changed fields: {}", batch.hostname, existing.id, changedFields.joinToString(", "))
   432→            existing
   433→        } else {
   434→            log.debug("No changes for asset '{}' (id={}), skipping database write", batch.hostname, existing.id)
   435→            existing
   436→        }
   437→    }
   438→}

   441→ * Result of importing vulnerabilities for a single server
   442→ */
   443→data class ServerImportResult(
   444→    val assetCreated: Boolean,
   445→    val vulnerabilitiesImported: Int,
   446→    val vulnerabilitiesSkipped: Int,
   447→    val vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate: Int
   448→)

    10→import io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable
    11→import io.micronaut.data.model.Sort

    13→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    14→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
    15→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory

    17→import reactor.core.publisher.Sinks
    18→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    19→import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit
    20→
    21→/**
    22→ * Service for managing materialized view refresh operations
    23→ *
    24→ * Provides asynchronous refresh of outdated assets materialized view with:
    25→ * - Background job execution (@Async)
    26→ * - Progress tracking and SSE event publishing
    27→ * - Batch processing for performance (1000 assets/chunk)
    28→ * - Error handling and audit trail
    29→ * - Observability metrics and structured logging
    30→ *
    31→ * Feature: 034-outdated-assets
    32→ * Task: T011
    33→ * Spec reference: FR-005, FR-007, FR-021, FR-022, research.md
    34→ */
    35→@Singleton
    36→open class MaterializedViewRefreshService(
    37→    private val refreshJobRepository: MaterializedViewRefreshJobRepository,
    38→    private val outdatedAssetRepository: OutdatedAssetMaterializedViewRepository,
    39→    private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository,
    40→    private val vulnerabilityConfigService: VulnerabilityConfigService,
    41→    private val eventPublisher: ApplicationEventPublisher<RefreshProgressEvent>,
    42→    private val vulnerabilityExceptionService: VulnerabilityExceptionService  // For checking active exceptions
    43→) {
    44→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MaterializedViewRefreshService::class.java)
    45→
    46→    // SSE sink for broadcasting refresh progress to all connected clients
    47→    // Many().multicast() allows multiple subscribers
    48→    private val progressSink: Sinks.Many<RefreshProgressEvent> = Sinks.many().multicast().onBackpressureBuffer()
    49→
    50→    companion object {
    51→        private const val BATCH_SIZE = 1000
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    /**
    55→     * Trigger asynchronous materialized view refresh with concurrency control
    56→     *
    57→     * Creates a refresh job and executes refresh in background.
    58→     * Returns immediately (non-blocking).
    59→     *
    60→     * **Concurrency Control (Performance Optimization):**
    61→     * If a refresh job is already running, this method returns the existing job
    62→     * instead of starting a new one. This prevents multiple concurrent refreshes
    63→     * from wasting resources when triggered by batch operations (e.g., CLI imports).
    64→     *
    65→     * Task: T011
    66→     * Spec reference: FR-005 (async refresh)
    67→     *
    68→     * @param triggeredBy Description of what triggered the refresh (for audit trail)
    69→     * @return Either the newly created job, or the existing running job (if one is in progress)
    70→     */
    71→    fun triggerAsyncRefresh(triggeredBy: String): MaterializedViewRefreshJob {
    72→        log.info("Triggering async refresh: triggeredBy={}", triggeredBy)
    73→
    74→        // Concurrency control: Check if a refresh is already running
    75→        val runningJob = getCurrentRunningJob()
    76→        if (runningJob != null) {
    77→            log.info("Skipping refresh trigger - job already running: jobId={}, triggeredBy={}, progress={}%",
    78→                runningJob.id, runningJob.triggeredBy, runningJob.progressPercentage)
    79→            return runningJob
    80→        }
    81→
    82→        // Create job entity
    83→        val job = MaterializedViewRefreshJob(
    84→            triggeredBy = triggeredBy,
    85→            totalAssets = 0  // Will be calculated during refresh
    86→        )
    87→        val savedJob = refreshJobRepository.save(job)
    88→
    89→        // Execute refresh asynchronously
    90→        executeRefreshAsync(savedJob.id!!)
    91→
    92→        return savedJob
    93→    }
    94→
    95→    /**
    96→     * Execute refresh in background thread
    97→     *
    98→     * Task: T011
    99→     * Spec reference: FR-005, FR-007, FR-021, FR-022
   100→     */
   101→    @Async
   102→    open fun executeRefreshAsync(jobId: Long) {
   103→        val job = refreshJobRepository.findById(jobId).orElseThrow()
   104→
   105→        try {
   106→            log.info("Starting async refresh job: jobId={}, triggeredBy={}", jobId, job.triggeredBy)
   107→
   108→            executeRefresh(job)
   109→
   110→            log.info("Async refresh job completed: jobId={}, durationMs={}", jobId, job.durationMs)
   111→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   112→            log.error("Async refresh job failed: jobId={}, error={}", jobId, e.message, e)
   113→            job.markFailed(e.message ?: "Unknown error")
   114→            refreshJobRepository.update(job)
   115→
   116→            // Publish failure event
   117→            publishProgressEvent(job, "Refresh failed: ${e.message}")
   118→        }
   119→    }
   120→
   121→    /**
   122→     * Execute materialized view refresh with progress tracking
   123→     *
   124→     * **Performance Optimization (Fix 3):**
   125→     * Uses batch query to load all overdue vulnerabilities in ONE database call,
   126→     * eliminating the N+1 query pattern where we previously queried per-asset.
   127→     *
   128→     * Task: T011, T060 (progress publishing), T061 (batch processing)
   129→     * Spec reference: FR-005, FR-007
   130→     *
   131→     * Note: NOT @Transactional - uses separate short transactions for each operation
   132→     * to avoid holding database locks during long-running refresh process
   133→     */
   134→    open fun executeRefresh(job: MaterializedViewRefreshJob) {
   135→        val thresholdDays = vulnerabilityConfigService.getReminderOneDays()
   136→        log.info("Executing refresh with threshold: {} days", thresholdDays)
   137→
   138→        // Step 1: Clear old materialized view data in separate short transaction
   139→        log.debug("Deleting old materialized view data")
   140→        clearMaterializedView()
   141→
   142→        // Step 2: Load ALL overdue vulnerabilities in ONE query (Fix 3 - eliminates N+1 pattern)
   143→        val thresholdDate = LocalDateTime.now().minusDays(thresholdDays.toLong())
   144→        log.debug("Fetching all vulnerabilities older than {} (threshold: {} days)", thresholdDate, thresholdDays)
   145→
   146→        val allOverdueVulnerabilities = vulnerabilityRepository.findOverdueVulnerabilitiesWithAssets(thresholdDate)
   147→        log.info("Loaded {} overdue vulnerabilities in single query", allOverdueVulnerabilities.size)
   148→
   149→        // Step 3: Group vulnerabilities by asset and filter exceptions
   150→        val vulnerabilitiesByAsset = allOverdueVulnerabilities.groupBy { it.asset }

     3→import com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonIgnore

     8→
     9→/**
    10→ * Vulnerability entity representing a security vulnerability discovered during a scan.
    11→ *
    12→ * Linked to an Asset entity via ManyToOne relationship.
    13→ * Feature 048: Uses transactional replace pattern - each import deletes existing vulnerabilities
    14→ * for an asset and inserts current ones, ensuring only the latest state is stored.
    15→ *
    16→ * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
    17→ * Related to: Feature 048-prevent-duplicate-vulnerabilities
    18→ *
    19→ * @property id Unique identifier
    20→ * @property asset Asset this vulnerability affects (required)
    21→ * @property vulnerabilityId CVE or vulnerability identifier (e.g., "CVE-2016-2183")
    22→ * @property cvssSeverity CVSS severity level (e.g., "High", "Critical", "Medium", "Low", "Informational")
    23→ * @property vulnerableProductVersions Affected product/version information
    24→ * @property daysOpen Text representation of days open (e.g., "58 days") - display only
    25→ * @property scanTimestamp Discovery date (calculated from daysOpen for accurate overdue calculation)
    26→ * @property importTimestamp When this data was imported (same for all vulns in batch, for "current" query)
    27→ * @property patchPublicationDate When the vendor published the patch/fix (optional, from CrowdStrike API)
    28→ * @property createdAt When the record was first created in the database (auto-generated)
    29→ */
    30→@Entity
    31→@Table(
    32→    name = "vulnerability",
    33→    indexes = [
    34→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_asset", columnList = "asset_id"),
    35→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_asset_scan", columnList = "asset_id,scan_timestamp"),
    36→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_severity", columnList = "cvss_severity"),
    37→        // Query optimization indexes (Feature: Database Structure Optimization)
    38→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_cve", columnList = "vulnerability_id"),                    // CVE ID lookups
    39→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_asset_cve", columnList = "asset_id,vulnerability_id"),     // Duplicate detection
    40→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_product", columnList = "vulnerable_product_versions"),     // Product filtering
    41→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_scan_timestamp", columnList = "scan_timestamp"),           // Temporal queries
    42→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_import_ts", columnList = "asset_id,import_timestamp"),      // "Current" queries
    43→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_sort_order", columnList = "import_timestamp DESC, vulnerability_id")  // ORDER BY optimization for paginated queries
    44→    ]
    45→)
    46→@Serdeable
    47→data class Vulnerability(
    48→    @Id
    49→    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    50→    var id: Long? = null,
    51→
    52→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
    53→    @JoinColumn(name = "asset_id", nullable = false)
    54→    @JsonIgnore // Prevent lazy loading errors during JSON serialization
    55→    @NotNull
    56→    var asset: Asset,
    57→
    58→    @Column(name = "vulnerability_id", length = 255)
    59→    var vulnerabilityId: String? = null,
    60→
    61→    @Column(name = "cvss_severity", length = 50)
    62→    var cvssSeverity: String? = null,
    63→
    64→    @Column(name = "vulnerable_product_versions", length = 512)
    65→    var vulnerableProductVersions: String? = null,
    66→
    67→    @Column(name = "days_open", length = 50)
    68→    var daysOpen: String? = null,
    69→
    70→    @Column(name = "scan_timestamp", nullable = false)
    71→    @NotNull
    72→    var scanTimestamp: LocalDateTime,
    73→
    74→    @Column(name = "patch_publication_date")
    75→    var patchPublicationDate: LocalDateTime? = null,
    76→
    77→    @Column(name = "import_timestamp")
    78→    var importTimestamp: LocalDateTime? = null,
    79→
    80→    @Column(name = "created_at", updatable = false)
    81→    var createdAt: LocalDateTime? = null
    82→) {
    83→    @PrePersist
    84→    fun onCreate() {
    85→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
    86→        createdAt = now
    87→        // Default importTimestamp to createdAt if not explicitly set
    88→        if (importTimestamp == null) {
    89→            importTimestamp = now
    90→        }
    91→    }
    92→
    93→    override fun toString(): String {
    94→        return "Vulnerability(id=$id, vulnerabilityId='$vulnerabilityId', " +
    95→                "cvssSeverity='$cvssSeverity', scanTimestamp=$scanTimestamp)"
    96→    }
    97→
    98→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
    99→        if (this === other) return true
   100→        if (other !is Vulnerability) return false
   101→        return id != null && id == other.id
   102→    }
   103→
   104→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
   105→        return id?.hashCode() ?: 0
   106→    }
   107→}
   108→

     9→/**

    13→ * Used by: CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto

    20→ */
    21→@Serdeable
    22→data class VulnerabilityDto(

   182→
   183→            // Save batch in separate transaction

   190→
   191→            // Publish progress event
   192→            publishProgressEvent(job, "Processing assets...")
   193→
   194→            log.debug("Processed batch {}: {} assets", batchIndex + 1, processed)
   195→        }
   196→
   197→        // Step 5: Mark job as completed
   198→        job.markCompleted()
   199→        updateJob(job)
   200→
   201→        // Publish completion event
